title: guettarda subcapitata
intro: Guettarda subcapitata, also known as the Jamaican dogwood, is a flowering plant species native to the Caribbean region. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce anxiety, insomnia, and other nervous system disorders due to the presence of a toxic compound called scutellarin. The health benefits of Guettarda subcapitata include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and even snake bites. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve boiling the bark or leaves to make a tea, which can be consumed for its medicinal effects.
